repo_prior_art.cite: pid_dose_response_iv_v1 finite LP envelope and pid_did_anticipation_bounded_v1 support-function DiD intervals are adjacent but not continuous-treatment DiD ACRT cross-dose ATT envelopes

published_prior_art.cite: CallawayGoodmanBaconSantAnna2021 defines the continuous-treatment DiD treated-type/ACRT selection-bias problem; Voronin2025 and ManskiPepper2000 supply generic LP/shape-restriction templates

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_dose_response_shape_v1 flagged the same law-level pasting risk for finite-grid shape envelopes, but outside the CGS ACRT array

published_prior_art.cite: ManskiPepper2000 gives nonparametric shape-restriction bounds; no verified paper gives law-sharp CGS ACRT Lipschitz/isotonic completion

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_dose_response_iv_v1 contains a critical-radius/sign-certificate pattern for a different continuous-IV dose-response target

published_prior_art.cite: ChernozhukovLeeRosen2013 and Stoye2020 cover optimized/intersection bounds and scalar interval inference, not an ACRT inverse Lipschitz sign frontier
KaidoMolinariStoye2019, BelloniBugniChernozhukov2018, Stoye2020, and Voronin2025 provide general projection/moment-inequality/LP-value inference templates

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The feasible set does not explicitly impose the definitional baseline constraints alpha_{0h}=0 for all h, yet the ACRT stencil may load alpha_{0h}, so the LP endpoints can depend on impossible baseline-dose effects.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: A_CGS and b_CGS(P) are not defined as explicit observable law functionals; 'any observed adjacent-dose DiD decomposition constraints used by the chosen stencil' leaves the core comparator input underspecified.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The 'active Lipschitz slack loading' denominator is not defined, so the affine-fractional frontier statement is not yet a well-posed mathematical claim.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Conjecture 1
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Law-sharpness is intentionally open and depends on a finite latent-completion construction.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The proposal is conditional on Stage 0 proving latent-law realization; Theorem 1 remains only algebraic until then.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Conjecture 3
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Inference is pointwise rather than uniform over laws.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Coverage claims are limited to pointwise validity under the stated sampling, active-face, and frontier-contact assumptions.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Three-dose audit
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Strict nonseparability is claimed only for the isotonic variant and more complex CGS contrast systems, not for every Lipschitz one-sided two-dose stencil.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The nonseparability exhibit is accepted only as an isotonic sanity witness unless a Lipschitz witness is added.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add alpha_{0h}=0 for every conditioning group h to the feasible polytope, then re-check Theorem 1 and both collapse corollaries with q_{0h} allowed to be nonzero.

Replace the vague A_CGS alpha=b_CGS(P) placeholder with the exact CGS observable contrast equations, including the section/equation anchor in CallawayGoodmanBaconSantAnna2021 and the derivation from their definitions.
Make Conjecture 2 an LP sensitivity statement with a defined active-set notation; if the frontier is actually piecewise affine rather than affine fractional, state that weaker but cleaner object.

Add D'Haultfoeuille-Hoderlein-Sasaki continuous-treatment DiD and the de Chaisemartin-Ciccia-D'Haultfoeuille-Knau no-true-control bounds paper as explicit adjacent comparisons or explain why their estimands differ.

recommended_next_step: Continue revision on this angle: fix the polytope definition and CGS contrast derivation before advancing to Stage 0.
